title: Abortion Pill Lawsuit Leaves Trump in a Political Bind Ahead of the Midterms
summary: Louisiana wants the Food and Drug Administration to curtail access to the medication. Doing so could cost Republicans at the polls.

title: Trump’s Approval Sinks Amid Unpopular War, Darkening G.O.P. Prospects
summary: With the midterms nearing, President Trump’s approval rating has hit a second-term low as voters question his handling of the economy, according to the latest New York Times/Siena poll.

title: Georgia Republicans Grasp for a Contender to Take On Democratic Sen. Jon Ossoff
summary: On the eve of their primary, Republicans have grown nervous about their prospects in November against Mr. Ossoff, regarded as the most vulnerable Democratic senator in the fall midterms.

title: What is Trump’s $1.776 Billion ‘Anti-Weaponization’ Fund?
summary: The Trump administration is creating a $1.8 billion fund to compensate people it says were wronged by the federal government, a group that could be largely made up of the president’s allies.

title: ICE Agent Charged in Shooting of a Venezuelan Immigrant in Minnesota
summary: The agent is the second federal officer to face felony charges in Minnesota stemming from Operation Metro Surge, the Trump administration’s immigration crackdown.

title: Tonys 2026 Predictions: Who Will Win? And Who Should?
summary: Our chief theater critic looks at this year’s nominees and makes some predictions (and recommendations).

title: He’s Been Impeached and Indicted. He Has a Chance in Texas’s Senate Race.
summary: The fate of Ken Paxton, the conservative Texas firebrand, could decide whether Republicans keep control of the Senate.

title: Ebola Was Identified in Congo Weeks Before W.H.O. Declared an Emergency
summary: Early surveillance and testing failed to identify Bundibugyo, a rare species of Ebola responsible for the current outbreak.

title: What to Know About the Latest Ebola Outbreak
summary: The World Health Organization declared a global health emergency after about 80 people died from the virus in the Democratic Republic of Congo.

title: In Closed-Door Talks, U.S. Demands a Major Role in Greenland
summary: Greenlandic officials worry about the direction of the negotiations aimed at defusing President Trump’s threats to seize their island. But they have little leverage.

title: Trump’s Taiwan Gambit is Already a Gift to China
summary: President Trump’s open willingness to hold up a $14 billion Taiwan arms package is a win for Beijing. Now China could be weighing how to keep the weapons on ice for as long as it can.

title: Actually, Democracy Dies in H.R.
summary: New research sheds light on how mediocre employees help would-be authoritarians maintain power.

title: Trump Warns Iran the ‘Clock Is Ticking’ as Tensions Surge Again in Middle East
summary: The president has sought to force Iran to accept his terms on its nuclear program or else face renewed war. An emboldened Iran has rebuffed Trump’s demands.

title: Blocked Strait of Hormuz Leads to New Opportunities for Syria
summary: The latest war in the Middle East has created new economic opportunities for Syria thanks to its geography.

title: Gun Found in Mangione’s Backpack Can be Used as Evidence, Judge Says
summary: The ruling was a partial victory for prosecutors in the murder trial of Luigi Mangione. A state judge ruled that some other material and statements could not be used.

title: Mamdani’s First City-Owned Grocery Store Is Planned for the South Bronx
summary: Mayor Zohran Mamdani said that the store would open in the Hunts Point neighborhood next year. Some merchants are pushing back.

title: Trump Has Done More Than Misjudge Iran
summary: The war has revealed the true puniness of Trump’s power

title: Catastrophe Is Emerging in the World’s Most Vulnerable Places
summary: The humanitarian relief system, decimated by cuts, faces a grave challenge as the Middle East war causes soaring costs for food, fuel and fertilizer.

title: Two Military Jets Collide in Midair at Idaho Air Show
summary: Video shared on social media showed the jets striking each other and spinning toward the ground. Four crew members safely ejected before the planes crashed, officials said.

title: New York Times Sues Pentagon for a Second Time
summary: The Times is challenging a new requirement that reporters covering the military complex have an official escort, part of a broader legal challenge to the Pentagon’s press restrictions.

title: Millions Recovered in FIFA Corruption Scandal May Now Be Missing Again Before World Cup
summary: Ahead of the World Cup, the head of South American soccer faces an ethics complaint he received payments recovered from a 2015 investigation that shook global soccer.
